instruction: Questions 11–16: Choose A, B or C. Questions 17–20: Matching A–F.

questions.prompt: Annie recommends that when cross-country skiing the visitors should

options.label: get away from the regular trails.
stop to enjoy views of the scenery.
go at a slow speed at the beginning.

questions.sectionInstruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.prompt: What does Annie tell the group about this afternoon's dog-sled trip?

options.label: Those who want to can take part in a race.
Anyone has the chance to drive a team of dogs.
One group member will be chosen to lead the trail.

questions.prompt: What does Annie say about the team relay event?

options.label: All participants receive a medal.
The course is 4 km long.
Each team is led by a teacher.

questions.prompt: On the snow-shoe trip, the visitors will

options.label: visit an old gold mine.
learn about unusual flowers.
climb to the top of a mountain.

questions.prompt: The cost of accommodation in the mountain hut includes

options.label: a supply of drinking water.
transport of visitors' luggage.
cooked meals.

questions.prompt: If there is a storm while the visitors are in the hut, they should

options.label: contact the bus driver.
wait until the weather improves.
use the emergency locator beacon.
It has a good place to stop and rest.
It is suitable for all abilities.
It involves crossing a river.
It demands a lot of skill.
It may be closed in bad weather.
It has some very narrow sections.

questions.sectionInstruction: What information does Annie give about skiing on each of the following mountain trails? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to Questions 17–20.
